what_it_does: Defines a static HTML design sheet for a v6 house-ruled decision card, showing four phone mockups: binary decision, multi-option decision, decided state, and error state.
observations: Imports Plus Jakarta Sans and IBM Plex Mono from Google Fonts plus shared piece.css and phone.css styles.
Uses a warm radial background on .screen.decision-card with CSS custom properties from the wider surface system.
Presents decision options as ledger-like rows with dots, labels, impact text, recommendation text, and a Take value area.
Shows selected and recommended states with gold wash, gold dot, and inset gold rule.
Includes centered decided and error states with large status marks, heading text, supporting line, and bottom actions.
Injects compass icons into context headings through DNA.org.icon from /surface/runtime/organisms.js.
standouts: Four states are colocated in one file for direct visual comparison.
The option rows carry decision metadata through label, impact, recommendation, ordinal dot, and action text.
The file comments document the intended visual register and state roles in detail.
conventions: Uses shared phone chrome classes such as .screen, .sbar, .p-hd, .p-sup, .p-h, and .p-hatch.
Uses .dc- prefixed selectors for file-local decision-card styles.
Uses CSS variables for color, spacing, radius, and type scale rather than hard-coded design values in most places.
Uses inline comments as design rationale alongside implementation CSS.
Uses div-based mockup controls rather than semantic button or form elements.
concerns: The recommended label is placed before the ordinal dot in the first option rows, while the CSS comment says it sits inline in the value column.
The option rows and action controls look interactive but are div elements with no local click handlers or semantic controls.
The decided screen uses an inline style on the ghost action for bottom positioning even though .dc-ghost already sets that value.
notes: A compact decision-card design specimen showing option selection, completion, and failure states in the same house-ruled visual language.
questions: Is this intended to remain a static visual substrate, or should the rows and actions become interactive controls?
Should the recommended label visually live in the value column as the comment says, or at the start of the row as the markup places it?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it describes and renders a house-ruled decision card with ledger rows, recommendation styling, thumb-reach actions, decided feedback, and error feedback.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to define a reusable decision-card visual pattern where choices, recommendation, confirmation, and recovery are all expressed through the same restrained phone UI substrate.
novelty: The distinct element is the combination of ledger rows, metal-toned ordinal dots, recommendation labeling, and centered bind/error moments inside a phone mockup design specimen.
contribution: Contributes a decision-card substrate with warm explanatory background, context framing, ledger-style option rows, recommendation and selected-state treatments, bottom primary and secondary actions, and compact decided/error state patterns.
summary_for_embedding: Static HTML/CSS design specimen for a house-ruled decision card with binary, multi-option, decided, and error phone mockups using warm radial grounding and ledger-like choice rows.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for expressing a decision workflow as a visual substrate with context, recommended choices, thumb-reach actions, and clear completion or retry states.
